title: 9 High-Dividend Energy Stocks That Could Soar on the Back of Their Q1 Results
author: David Wagner
description: Oil prices have dropped from March peaks but remain elevated due to Iran conflict uncertainties and Strait of Hormuz vulnerabilities. Energy companies are positioned to report strong Q1 2026 earnings with sustained high oil prices. Nine undervalued US energy stocks with 2-4% dividend yields are identified as having 20-40% upside potential based on analyst price targets.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Large independent natural gas producer benefiting from strong LNG demand due to global energy disruptions. Q1 2026 EPS expected at $3.67 (up sharply YoY) with full-year EPS projected at $8.97. Most analysts rate it a buy.

Strengthened position through Civitas Resources merger, expanding presence in low-cost Permian Basin assets. High oil prices expected to support strong profitability. Earnings estimates revised higher in recent weeks.

One of the largest global exploration and production players with diversified operations across multiple regions. Analysts expect steady earnings growth in 2026 supported by firm oil prices and cost discipline. However, article notes other stocks show higher potential.

title: Oil Above $90, Pump Above $4 — And 7 Energy Stocks Still Trading At A Wide Discount
author: Piero Cingari
description: Seven major energy stocks are trading at historically low valuations (7x-11x forward P/E) despite oil prices above $90/barrel due to the Strait of Hormuz crisis. The sector has underperformed crude oil gains, creating a potential opportunity if the supply disruption persists, though risks remain if a ceasefire rapidly brings prices back down to $65-70.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Low-breakeven producer positioned to benefit from sustained $90+ oil prices, with 9.5x forward P/E and 12.2% analyst upside potential.
Low-breakeven producer trading at 11.3x forward P/E with 13.9% analyst upside, positioned to capture free cash flow from sustained high oil prices.
Trading at the lowest forward P/E (7.2x) among large-cap producers with 49.6% YTD gains, representing significant undervaluation relative to oil price strength and supply disruption environment.
Trading at 8.6x forward earnings with median analyst target implying 31.6% upside, showing the most analyst conviction despite already strong performance.
Carries widest analyst upside at 38.5% despite being the only name still negative YTD, suggesting significant recovery potential if supply concerns persist.
Refiner positioned to benefit from elevated crack spreads (margin between crude and refined products) with 10.7x forward P/E and 37.1% YTD gains.
Refiner with 11.2x forward P/E benefiting from widened crack spreads in elevated crude environment, with 44.4% YTD gains.
Up 27% YTD but underperformed crude oil's 38% gain, indicating sector-wide undervaluation relative to commodity strength; represents both opportunity and risk depending on Hormuz resolution.

title: Disciplined Growth Investors Trim InterDigital After Strong Run in Wireless Technology Stock
author: Eric Trie
description: Disciplined Growth Investors reduced its stake in InterDigital by 181,788 shares (worth $64.27 million) after the wireless technology patent licensing company's stock surged 70.3% over the past year. The fund's position declined from 4.947% to 3.71% of assets under management, though InterDigital remains a significant holding at $193.94 million. The company develops and licenses wireless patents for 5G, IoT, and video coding technologies.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: While the stock has performed exceptionally well (up 70.3% over the past year), the fund's decision to trim the position suggests profit-taking after strong gains rather than fundamental concerns. The company's business model and market position remain solid, but the reduction indicates valuation-driven rebalancing rather than negative outlook.
Mentioned as a top holding of the fund ($282.09 million, 5.4% of AUM) with no specific news or changes reported. Neutral sentiment reflects its status as a stable portfolio position.
Listed as a top holding ($281.40 million, 5.4% of AUM) with no specific news or changes reported. Neutral sentiment reflects its status as a stable portfolio position.

title: Azul Resources II Announces Commitment from Carnelian Energy Capital
author: Na
description: Azul Resources II, led by Zach Hart and the team from predecessor Azul Resources, has closed an equity commitment from Carnelian Energy Capital Management. The company will continue acquiring and developing assets in the Haynesville and Bossier Shale plays of East Texas and North Louisiana, leveraging the team's proven track record of building and operating natural gas assets in the region.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Mentioned only as the acquirer of Indigo Natural Resources (where Azul's team previously worked). No direct business impact or news related to Expand Energy is discussed in the article.

title: Oil Outlook Looks Ugly—That’s Why These 3 Energy Plays Matter
author: Nathan Reiff
description: The U.S. Energy Information Administration expects crude oil supply glut to persist through 2026, potentially pushing prices to $55/barrel in Q1. Despite near-term headwinds for oil and gas stocks, three lesser-known energy companies present value opportunities for risk-tolerant investors: Expand Energy (LNG producer with strong cost management), Mach Natural Resources (shifting toward natural gas with low valuation), and Archrock (equipment supplier benefiting from secular energy demand).

insights.sentiment_reasoning: LNG producer underperformed market YTD (6.5% vs S&P 500's 17.2%) but beat earnings estimates, reduced debt by $1.2B, and analysts expect EPS to quadruple to $5.48 with 20% upside potential. 19 of 22 analysts rate it a Buy.

LNG producer underperformed market YTD (6.5% vs S&P 500's 17.2%) but beat earnings estimates, reduced debt by $1.2B, and analysts expect EPS to quadruple to $5.48 with 20% upside potential. 19 of 22 analysts rate it a Buy.

LNG producer underperformed market YTD (6.5% vs S&P 500's 17.2%) but beat earnings estimates, reduced debt by $1.2B, and analysts expect EPS to quadruple to $5.48 with 20% upside potential. 19 of 22 analysts rate it a Buy.

LNG producer underperformed market YTD (6.5% vs S&P 500's 17.2%) but beat earnings estimates, reduced debt by $1.2B, and analysts expect EPS to quadruple to $5.48 with 20% upside potential. 19 of 22 analysts rate it a Buy.

Equipment supplier showing resilience with ~50% YoY improvement in adjusted EPS, excellent fleet utilization, and raised EBITDA guidance. Multiple dividend increases demonstrate commitment to shareholder returns despite only 1% YTD return.
